sector: Semiconductors — magnetic sensing (Hall/TMR) + power ICs (isolated SiC/GaN gate drivers, current sensors); end-markets automotive (xEV/ADAS), industrial, and a fast-ramping AI data-center power/sensing vertical.
bull_thesis:
Allegro MicroSystems is a high-quality, intellectual-property-rich sensing franchise that has added a genuine AI-infrastructure optionality leg on top of a recovering content-growth core. In fiscal year 2026, the company posted revenue of $890 million, up 23% year over year, with the fourth quarter alone reaching $243 million (up 26%). Non-GAAP gross margin (revenue minus cost of goods sold, before certain non-cash charges) came in at 49.4% for the full year and 50.0% in the fourth quarter — a 140-basis-point improvement year over year despite a roughly 200-basis-point gold-cost headwind. Operating margin reached 14.1% for the year and 15.6% in the fourth quarter. Non-GAAP earnings per share of $0.54 for the year more than doubled year over year. Free cash flow hit a record $125 million, the company voluntarily paid down $60 million of debt, and net debt stands at only about $116 million — less than 0.7 times earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortisation (EBITDA). The term loan was repriced 25 basis points lower to SOFR plus 175 basis points.

The new data-center business is the headline growth driver. It quadrupled in fiscal 2026 to roughly 10% of full-year sales, climbed to 14% of fourth-quarter sales (up 41% sequentially), and management guided it to reach 16–17% of first-quarter fiscal 2027 sales, implying 20–25% sequential growth. Within the data-center segment, current sensors went from virtually zero in fiscal year 2023 to about 20% of data-center revenue by the fourth quarter. That growth is coming from Allegro's core magnetic-sensing intellectual property being applied to 800-volt high-voltage direct-current (HVDC) artificial-intelligence racks — the company is leveraging an existing advantage rather than entering a commodity power-switching market it does not own. Management's stated content-per-rack opportunity is $150 today rising to over $425 in next-generation AI configurations.

The legacy business is also strengthening. Total automotive revenue rose 17%, well above the roughly 7–10% industry unit-production growth rate. Focused Automotive (electric vehicles and advanced driver-assistance systems) grew 30% to $349 million. Industrial was up 38%. Allegro's tunneling-magnetoresistance (TMR) technology leadership — including the industry-first 10 MHz TMR current sensor and the EDN 2025 Sensor Product of the Year award — and its isolated silicon-carbide (SiC) gate drivers each carry a stated 2–3 times content-per-vehicle uplift as customers move to 800-volt electric vehicles and steer-by-wire architectures. Design wins grew 30% year over year and the order backlog is at a multi-year high. Two Chinese humanoid-robotics design wins seed the next potential growth leg. If the data-center ramp converts and the automotive market restocks, Allegro can compound revenue at a mid-teens-plus rate while gross margins climb toward 52%.
bear_thesis:
The business is excellent; the price has already paid for the optionality. Allegro trades at around $59 per share — roughly an $11 billion market capitalisation — implying a forward price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio of about 56 times, an enterprise value (EV, or market cap plus net debt) divided by sales of roughly 12.5 times, and an EV divided by trailing EBITDA above 100 times. The stock soared alongside Monolithic Power Systems in June 2026. That multiple requires a triple: the data-center content opportunity converts at scale, the automotive cycle re-accelerates, and margins keep climbing. Three problems stand in the way.

First, the data-center segment is only one fiscal year old, revenue has been lumpy, and it depends entirely on hyperscaler capital-expenditure budgets — a digestion pause would hit the highest-multiple part of the story first. Second, Allegro is not a named anchor on NVIDIA's published 800-volt direct-current (DC) ecosystem partner roster, which includes Infineon, Navitas, Analog Devices, Renesas, STMicroelectronics, and Texas Instruments; Allegro ships components into the architecture and competes in a more contested power layer against larger, better-funded rivals including ON Semiconductor, Infineon, and Texas Instruments. Third, management itself acknowledged 'somewhat thin inventory in automotive' with 'no clear signs of restocking at a broad level' — 71% of revenue is recovering off a trough, not booming, so an automotive air-pocket would undercut the base while the data-center multiple remains elevated.

The thesis is not independent of a single macro variable: the marginal growth and the premium multiple both hinge on AI capital-expenditure durability — the same factor driving the rest of the basket. There is little margin of safety; this is a quality name at a price where the asymmetry points to the downside.
sop_bull: Sum-of-the-parts (valuing each business line separately) bull-case target: $72–78 per share. The core automotive plus industrial segment — roughly $762 million of fiscal 2026 revenue, growing at an estimated 15–18% with gross margins rising toward 50% — is assigned approximately 9–10 times EV/sales given the sensing-IP moat and content compounding, producing an enterprise value of roughly $7.0–7.6 billion. The data-center segment — annualising at over $135 million exiting fiscal 2026, guided to 16–17% of a growing revenue base, growing at 40%-plus, and mapped to 800-volt HVDC racks — is assigned a premium of roughly 18–22 times EV/sales as a hyperscaler-power optionality, adding approximately $2.6–3.2 billion of enterprise value. Robotics and humanoid optionality plus debt-paydown credit add roughly $0.4–0.6 billion. Summing those pieces produces a total enterprise value of about $10.0–11.4 billion; subtracting $116 million of net debt and dividing by 186 million shares yields roughly $53–61 per share in the base scenario, with the bull data-center ramp pushing the blended fair value to roughly $72–78 if fiscal 2027 data-center revenue reaches about 20% of sales at a sustained premium multiple and automotive restocks. This is equivalent to roughly 50–55 times an estimated fiscal 2028 earnings power of about $1.40–1.50 per share.
sop_bear: Sum-of-the-parts bear-case target: $38–44 per share. This reflects a re-rating toward quality-cyclical-peer multiples once AI capital-expenditure optionality is discounted. Applying roughly 22–26 times a normalised fiscal 2027 non-GAAP earnings-per-share estimate of $0.85–1.00 — assuming a soft automotive market, no broad restock, and decelerating data-center growth as hyperscalers digest — yields equity value of about $40 per share divided by 186 million shares net of net debt. An EV/sales cross-check at roughly 6–7 times approximately $1.0 billion in fiscal 2027 sales produces an enterprise value of $6.5–7.0 billion; subtracting $116 million of net debt and dividing by 186 million shares gives roughly $34–37. Blending those approaches produces a $38–44 range. This is roughly where Allegro would settle if it re-rated toward ON Semiconductor (approximately 32–38 times forward earnings) or NXP Semiconductors (approximately 19 times forward earnings) rather than the Monolithic Power nosebleed cohort (approximately 50 times forward earnings) — representing a drawdown of about 30–35% from the current $59 price.
sop_breakdown: Base-case blended fair value: $52–58 per share. The base case combines a high-quality sensing-IP core with a discounted — but not zero-weighted — data-center optionality. The core automotive plus industrial segment is valued at roughly 8.5 times EV/sales on approximately $770 million of estimated fiscal 2027 core revenue, producing about $6.5 billion of enterprise value. The data-center segment is valued at roughly 16 times EV/sales on approximately $160 million of estimated fiscal 2027 data-center revenue, adding about $2.6 billion of enterprise value — a premium for 800-volt/AI exposure, haircut for one year of history plus non-anchor status on NVIDIA's roster plus capital-expenditure sensitivity. Robotics and other optionality plus a net-cash-trajectory credit add about $0.4 billion. Total enterprise value of roughly $9.5–10.5 billion, less $116 million of net debt, divided by 186 million shares, rounds to a $52–58 fair-value band — close to the current $59 quote. This implies roughly 36–40 times an estimated fiscal 2028 earnings power near $1.40 per share: a premium to ON Semiconductor and NXP, a discount to Monolithic Power, and appropriate for the quality and the still-unproven durability of the data-center business.
risk_reward: Poor to balanced. At roughly $59 per share, the upside to fair value is approximately negative 5% to negative 2% — the price is already at or above the $52–58 base-case band. The upside to the bull case of $72–78 is roughly 22–32%, but that requires the full triple: data-center converts at scale, automotive restocks, and margins keep climbing. The downside to the bear case of $38–44 is roughly 25–36% — the scenario that plays out if AI capital-expenditure digests or the automotive market disappoints and triggers a multiple re-rating. The risk-reward skew is roughly one times up versus 1.3 times down. Asymmetry points to the downside because the optionality is already priced in and the thesis is not independent of the broader AI-capex cycle.
catalyst_status: PENDING_HARD (with a partly-FIRED component). The load-bearing catalyst is the data-center capacity ramp: DC went 10% FY26 -> 14% Q4 (+41% q/q) -> GUIDED 16-17% of Q1 FY2027 sales (20-25% sequential) per the Q4 FY2026 release (FY ended Mar 27, 2026). That ramp is real, dated, and still inflecting forward (PENDING_HARD). BUT the AI-capex re-rate is partly already FIRED — ALGM 'soared' alongside MPWR in June 2026 and the multiple (fwd P/E ~56x, EV/Sales ~12.5x) already discounts the optionality. Verification gap: ALGM is NOT named on NVIDIA's published 800-VDC ecosystem partner roster (Infineon, Navitas, ADI, Renesas, STMicro, TI are named, per Fierce Sensors, 2026); ALGM ships current sensors (ACS37017/ACS37200) and Power-Thru SiC gate drivers INTO the 800V architecture (allegromicro.com / GlobeNewswire, Jan 2026) but no singular hyperscaler design-win award is publicly confirmed. So: real ramp, no anchor-award catalyst, and the re-rate is largely spent.
peer_comps_note:
True peers within the scale-out power-semiconductor and sensing layer (live multiples verified June 2026): Monolithic Power Systems is the closest valuation benchmark and the AI-power darling — roughly $1,566 per share, forward P/E roughly 50 times, next-twelve-month EV divided by EBITDA roughly 49 times (versus a peer median of roughly 21 times); Allegro sits in the same nosebleed cohort at a forward P/E of roughly 56 times, meaning Allegro is priced alongside the most expensive name in the group. ON Semiconductor is a direct power and silicon-carbide competitor for 800-volt applications — forward P/E roughly 32–38 times, EV/EBITDA roughly 15 times; cheaper, more cyclical, larger. NXP Semiconductors is an automotive and industrial analogue peer — forward P/E roughly 19 times, EV/EBITDA roughly 17 times — the value anchor of the cohort. Infineon, Texas Instruments, Analog Devices, Renesas, and STMicroelectronics are the named NVIDIA 800-volt DC partners and Allegro's true power-layer competitors: broader product lines, deeper pockets.

Ranking summary: Allegro is in the top tier on valuation richness (alongside Monolithic Power, at roughly 2.5–3 times NXP's multiple); strong on growth (up 23% in fiscal 2026, data-center revenue quadrupling); good and recovering on margins (gross margin roughly 50%, operating margin roughly 14–16% — below Monolithic Power's roughly 58% gross margin but improving quickly); mid-tier on returns (return on invested capital normalising off a trough, not yet durably above peers); and high but non-anchor on AI capital-expenditure exposure (real data-center ramp, but the company ships components into the architecture rather than being a named NVIDIA partner). Net assessment: Allegro is priced like Monolithic Power but carries competitive exposure in its fastest-growing layer that is more like ON Semiconductor's.
role_in_scaleout: Allegro supplies precision current-sensing silicon (using Hall-effect and tunneling-magnetoresistance technologies) and isolated silicon-carbide gate drivers that meter and switch power inside 800-volt DC artificial-intelligence racks and the upstream power-conversion chain. This is a real but non-exclusive picks-and-shovels role in the electrical-distribution and 800-volt power-semiconductor bottleneck. Demand is durable as a build-out beneficiary of both electrification and AI power growth, but the revenue is cyclically sensitive to capital-expenditure budgets and contested by larger power-semiconductor incumbents. The role rates as medium criticality and good-but-not-essential durability.
consensus_delta: Where we disagree with the market: we do not see a hidden mispricing in our favour. The market is looking directly at the data-center story and has bid the stock accordingly — forward P/E roughly 56 times, EV/sales roughly 12.5 times, and a sharp run alongside Monolithic Power in June 2026. If anything, the consensus error runs the other way: the multiple now assumes (1) the one-year-old data-center business converts the '$150 to $425 per rack' content opportunity at scale, (2) automotive restocks even though management explicitly said there are 'no clear signs of restocking at a broad level,' and (3) gross margins keep climbing toward 52%. That is a triple priced as a near-certainty. The most factually exposed consensus assumption is that Allegro is an anchor beneficiary of NVIDIA's 800-volt DC architecture — NVIDIA's published partner roster names Infineon, Navitas, Analog Devices, Renesas, STMicroelectronics, and Texas Instruments, not Allegro; Allegro ships components into the architecture and competes in the contested power layer. The expectations gap is unfavourable: there is little daylight between the current price and even the bull-case sum-of-the-parts.
valley_of_death: There is no balance-sheet cliff. Net debt is only about $116 million (less than 0.7 times EBITDA), free cash flow was a record $125 million, and the term loan was just repriced lower — solvency is a non-issue. The near-term risk (three to nine months) is a multiple event, not a cash event. Three scenarios define the risky window: (1) any hyperscaler capital-expenditure digestion headline, or an AI-capex 'guide-down' from a major cloud customer, would de-rate the highest-multiple data-center segment first; (2) the automotive end-market is recovering from a trough with thin inventory and no broad restock — an automotive air-pocket would undercut 71% of revenue while the data-center multiple remains extended; (3) the gold-cost headwind and the climb toward a gross margin above 52% leave little room for a margin miss against a 50–51% guide. The most likely path through this risky window is simply mean-reversion of a stock that has already run, triggered by the first data-center-growth deceleration print or an automotive disappointment.
positioning_washout: Allegro pays no dividend, so there is no yield-seeking or passive base to flush out. The holder base is growth and AI-momentum institutions (roughly 76% institutional ownership) — the cohort that bought the June 2026 melt-up. That cuts the wrong way for a new long entry: there is no forced-selling pressure to buy into. Instead, the risk is a momentum-unwind or portfolio de-grossing if AI-capex sentiment cracks (the macro regime document flags an 'AI-capex break' as a key tripwire, with stretched positioning and Bank of America Fund Manager Survey cash at 3.9%, leaving little dry powder). The forced-selling pressure to wait for would be an AI de-grossing flush that resets the multiple, not a structural shareholder rotation.
forcing_function: The catalyst that forces the re-rating is the quarterly data-center-mix print. First-quarter fiscal 2027 results (expected late July or early August 2026) must validate the guided 16–17% data-center mix and 20–25% sequential data-center growth; second-quarter fiscal 2027 is the follow-on confirmation. A clean beat that confirms data-center durability extends the premium; any sequential data-center deceleration or an automotive-restock miss is the event that forces a re-rating toward the ON Semiconductor or NXP cohort. There is no singular binding catalyst — no confirmed hyperscaler award, no deal closing, no proxy vote — so the re-rating driver is recurring earnings prints. That is precisely why the asymmetry is thin: the good news is already in the tape, and each earnings print is a fresh opportunity to disappoint.
moderator_conclusion:
Pass for now, conviction 2 out of 5. Allegro is a genuinely high-quality, well-run franchise: revenue accelerating at 23% in fiscal 2026, margins expanding against a real gold-cost headwind, record free cash flow, active deleveraging, a defensible magnetic-sensing intellectual-property core (tunneling-magnetoresistance leadership), and a real, fast-ramping AI data-center leg (data-center revenue growing from 10% of full-year sales to 14% of the fourth quarter, guided to 16–17% of the first quarter of fiscal 2027) that leverages that core IP into 800-volt HVDC racks. Forensic quality is high (Interrogator score 4 out of 5, trajectory strengthening, management candid and credible). But this is a regime-and-valuation pass, not a quality pass.

The macro regime rewards hard-dated, resolution-independent catalysts and penalises already-run AI-capex names whose thesis hinges on a single shared factor saturating the basket. Allegro is exactly that name. It soared alongside Monolithic Power in June 2026 to a forward P/E of roughly 56 times and an EV/sales of roughly 12.5 times. It is not a named NVIDIA 800-volt DC anchor partner (Infineon, Navitas, Analog Devices, Renesas, STMicroelectronics, and Texas Instruments are). Its data-center business is one fiscal year old and sensitive to capital-expenditure cycles. The base-case sum-of-the-parts fair value of $52–58 sits at or below the current $59 price. Risk-reward skews to the downside (roughly positive 25–30% bull versus negative 25–35% bear), and the catalyst is a hard, contractual-quality forward data that is already largely reflected in the share price. Conviction on the value case is also 2: even stripping away the AI overlay, investors are paying a premium-to-fair-value multiple for a narrow-moat, cyclically-recovering business with no margin of safety. The right behaviour is to watchlist, not buy: revisit on an AI-capex de-grossing flush that resets the multiple toward the ON Semiconductor or NXP cohort, or on a print that confirms data-center durability at a cheaper entry point. As things stand, the optionality is in the price and the thesis is not independent of the rest of the basket.
interrogator_dossier:
Interrogator Forensic Dossier — Allegro MicroSystems (ALGM)

Prepared as of 2026-06-22. Source: fourth-quarter and full-year fiscal 2026 earnings release and call (fiscal year ended March 27, 2026), first- through fourth-quarter fiscal 2026 commentary, and live fundamentals and valuation. Transcript sourced from the web. ALGM is a normally-listed Nasdaq equity.

Narrative Arc

Allegro entered fiscal 2026 positioned as an automotive and industrial magnetic-sensor and power-integrated-circuit supplier levered to electric-vehicle content growth — a unit-production-plus-content story. Across fiscal 2026 the story acquired a second engine: the data-center vertical went from a rounding error to the headline. Framing migrated from 'we outgrow vehicle production by 7–10 points on content' to a dual narrative: (1) the durable automotive and industrial content compounder, plus (2) an emergent, fast-ramping AI data-center power and sensing franchise. Data-center revenue quadrupled in fiscal 2026 to roughly 10% of total sales, climbed to 14% of fourth-quarter sales (up 41% sequentially), and management guided it to 16–17% of first-quarter fiscal 2027 sales (up 20–25% sequentially). Within data center, current sensors went from virtually zero entering fiscal 2023 to roughly 20% of data-center revenue by the fourth quarter — mapping directly onto NVIDIA's 800-volt DC architecture roadmap. The load-bearing content claim is '$150 per rack today, rising to over $425 in next-generation AI configurations.' The legacy business strengthened simultaneously: total automotive revenue up 17%, Focused Automotive (electric vehicles and advanced driver-assistance systems) up 30% to $349 million, industrial up 38%; tunneling-magnetoresistance sensing (roughly 30% of new product releases, including the industry-first 10 MHz TMR current sensor) and isolated silicon-carbide gate drivers each carry a stated 2–3 times content-per-vehicle uplift. Genuine expansion, not substitution.

Claims vs Financials

Revenue re-accelerating: full-year fiscal 2026 revenue $890 million (up 23%); fourth-quarter revenue $243 million (up 26% year over year, up 6% sequentially); first-quarter fiscal 2027 guidance $245–255 million — verified. Margins recovering: fourth-quarter non-GAAP gross margin 50.0% versus 45.6% in the prior-year fourth quarter; full-year non-GAAP gross margin 49.4% (up 140 basis points year over year despite a roughly 200-basis-point gold headwind); first-quarter fiscal 2027 gross-margin guidance 50–51% — verified and impressive. Operating leverage: full-year operating margin 14.1%, fourth-quarter 15.6%; non-GAAP earnings per share $0.54 for the full year (more than doubling year over year), $0.17 in the fourth quarter (up 183% year over year) — verified. Cash position and debt reduction: full-year free cash flow a record $125 million; $60 million voluntary debt repayment; year-end net debt $116 million; term loan repriced 25 basis points lower to SOFR plus 175 basis points — verified, clean balance sheet. Multi-year-high backlog and 30% design-win growth: plausible, partly unverifiable (management-defined metrics). Data center as a durable new business: the ramp is verified; durability is unproven (one fiscal year of history, lumpy, capital-expenditure-dependent). The narrative is richer than the trajectory supports on the '$150 to $425' per-rack content framing, which is an opportunity description rather than booked revenue. Credibility green flag: management proactively flagged 'somewhat thin inventory in automotive' with 'no clear signs of restocking at a broad level.' Note: aggregators showing approximately $725 million in trailing-twelve-month revenue or GAAP losses reflect the fiscal 2025 trough; the fiscal 2026 release is the correct basis.

Tone and Confidence Trajectory

Rising — and earned by the reported numbers. Early-year language defended content-over-production-growth against a soft automotive tape; by the fourth quarter the chief executive leads with data-center records and a multi-year-high backlog, and the chief financial officer quantifies the current-sensor ramp. The shift from defensive (hold content share through a destock) to offensive (new addressable market, content-per-rack, robotics wins) is paired with a candid automotive-restock admission. Confident without bravado. No tonal red flags.

Guidance Credibility

Medium to high. Management delivered into a sequentially improving plan and beat into the fourth quarter. Margin trajectory hit precisely despite the gold headwind. The first-quarter fiscal 2027 guidance is internally consistent and not obviously sandbagged. Caveat: a rising share of the guidance leans on sequential data-center growth — a segment with only one year of history and high capital-expenditure sensitivity — so reliability is high but durability is less tested.

Analyst Pressure Points

Data-center durability and customer concentration (how much revenue depends on one or two hyperscaler programmes; design-win-to-revenue conversion timeline; whether the '$425 per rack' figure is contracted or aspirational; limited named-customer disclosure). Automotive restocking timing (management's own 'no clear signs of restocking'). Gold and input-cost pass-through and the gross-margin ceiling. China and robotics optionality (two humanoid-robotics wins, revenue not yet sized).

Red Flags and Green Flags

Positive signals: gross-margin expansion against a real cost headwind; honest automotive-restock caveat; record free cash flow plus voluntary deleveraging plus term-loan reprice; a genuinely new addressable market mapped to NVIDIA's 800-volt DC architecture; tunneling-magnetoresistance technology leadership. Negative signals: valuation has fully discounted the optionality (forward P/E roughly 56 times, EV/sales roughly 12.5 times, EV/EBITDA above 100 times trailing; stock soared alongside Monolithic Power in June 2026); single-factor dependence on AI capital-expenditure durability; data-center revenue only one year old and lumpy; automotive end-market still soft (71% of revenue recovering from a trough).

Hidden Signals

The chief financial officer's unprompted quantification of the current-sensor share of data-center revenue signals that the data-center story is fundamentally a current-sensing story — leveraging the core intellectual-property moat — which is more defensible than entering commodity power switching head-on. Operating expenses declining sequentially alongside 23% revenue growth is a quiet operating-leverage signal. Robotics and humanoid design wins seed the next optionality leg before the data-center segment is fully mature. The persistent 'vehicle production plus 7–10%' anchor in management language signals that the automotive tape is still soft and management wants to be valued on content.

Capital-Allocation Verdict

Trajectory strengthening. Accelerating revenue, expanding margins against a cost headwind, record free cash flow, active deleveraging, and a genuinely new fast-ramping addressable market on a durable sensing intellectual-property core. The single thing to weigh: the quality is real but the price has already paid for the optionality. At roughly 56 times forward earnings and roughly 12.5 times EV/sales, Allegro is priced for a triple — data-center converts at scale, automotive re-accelerates, and margins climb — and the marginal buyer is underwriting AI capital-expenditure durability, which is the shared basket factor. There is little margin of safety. Moat: narrow — genuine intangible and intellectual-property advantage plus design-in switching costs in magnetic sensing (Hall-effect and tunneling-magnetoresistance technologies), but contested in raw power switching versus ON Semiconductor, Infineon, and Texas Instruments, with returns cyclically recovering rather than durably above peers. Moat trend: stable — the sensing intellectual property is firm; the fastest-growing revenue dollars are in the more contested power layer. Secular threat: none — Allegro is a beneficiary of electrification and AI power build-out; the risk is cyclical (AI capital-expenditure digestion plus automotive cycle), not terminal.

Management credibility 4 of 5; trajectory strengthening; moat narrow but real; moat trend stable; secular threat none.
skeptic_corrections: (1) The bear's load-bearing 'NVIDIA non-anchor' claim is verified even more firmly than originally stated. NVIDIA's own developer blog announcing the 800-volt DC architecture names its silicon providers as Analog Devices, Infineon, Innoscience, Monolithic Power Systems, Navitas, ON Semiconductor, Renesas, ROHM, STMicroelectronics, and Texas Instruments. Allegro appears nowhere on NVIDIA's published roster. Allegro ships components into the architecture but is not a named ecosystem partner; the picks-and-shovels role is real but non-exclusive and contested by larger incumbents on the list. (2) Source caveat on the data-center mix guidance: the '16–17% of first-quarter fiscal 2027 sales, up 20–25% sequentially' figure is not in the filed 8-K or press release. The 8-K confirms only the fourth-quarter '14% of total sales' data-center figure and the $245–255 million / $0.19–0.23 first-quarter fiscal 2027 guidance. The 16–17% data-center-mix number comes from the earnings call, not a filed document; treat it as management commentary, not a hard filed guide. (3) Deleverage wording: the 8-K shows a $285 million term-loan refinance and $345 million of term-loan repayments during fiscal 2026 (cash $168.8 million versus long-term debt $285.7 million = net debt roughly $117 million, consistent with the approximately $116 million claim). The '$60 million voluntary paydown' framing represents the net of those flows, but the net-debt and clean-balance-sheet conclusion holds. None of these findings break the thesis; they reinforce the bear (non-anchor status confirmed) and tighten one over-precise catalyst figure.
skeptic_evidence: NVIDIA Developer Blog, 'NVIDIA 800 VDC Architecture Will Power the Next Generation of AI Factories' (2026) — silicon providers named: Analog Devices, Infineon, Innoscience, MPS, Navitas, OnSemi, Renesas, ROHM, STMicroelectronics, Texas Instruments; Allegro NOT named.

Allegro MicroSystems FY2026 8-K / press release, GlobeNewswire 2026-05-07 (FY ended Mar 27, 2026): FY net sales $890.1M (+23%), Q4 $243.2M (+26%), non-GAAP GM 49.4% FY / 50.0% Q4, non-GAAP EPS $0.54 FY / $0.17 Q4, FCF $124.9M; Data Center 'record 14% of total Q4 sales'.

Allegro FY2026 8-K (SEC EDGAR, algm-ex99_1, filed 2026-05-07): cash & equivalents $168.8M vs long-term debt $285.7M as of Mar 27, 2026 (net debt ~$117M); Q1 FY2027 guide $245-255M net sales / $0.19-0.23 non-GAAP EPS — the press release contains NO 16-17% DC-mix guidance figure.
Navitas/NVIDIA MGX 800-VDC partner ceremony, GlobeNewswire 2026-06-03 — names the NVIDIA MGX 800-VDC ecosystem partners; Allegro absent, corroborating non-anchor status.

scale.role: Magnetic sensing + power-conversion; DC a record 14% of Q4 sales, ramping.
scale.rationale: Real DC-converts ramp (record 14% of Q4 sales) but the larger base is cyclical auto-sensing, and ALGM is NOT a named NVIDIA 800V ecosystem partner (it ships components in, non-exclusively) — a second-order TACTICAL franchise. Skeptic verdict CONFIRMED_WITH_CORRECTIONS, and the corrections REINFORCE the bear (non-anchor confirmed; the 16-17% DC-mix guide is call color, not a filed figure), so a ~8pt haircut off a ~44 franchise. The AI re-rate partly fired; second-order, wait.
scale.demand_durability: Medium — DC ramp is real but auto-sensing is the larger, more cyclical base.
